_source: manual review of data/derived/roll_locations_regex.json hits, cross-referenced with obtained_perks.json + rolls.json (curator's log, ch 1-75)
_note: First-pass human review. For each tagged hit, verdict is one of: 'real_roll' (regex correctly caught a gacha roll), 'real_miss' (caught a roll that missed), 'real_acquisition' (caught the post-roll acquisition prose), 'real_constellation' (named the constellation in a roll context), 'false_positive' (matched non-gacha prose). Patterns observed at the bottom inform the next iteration of the regex.

tagged_samples.evidence: Setup prose for the first power-vision: 'three hundred flares of lightning ... all but one completely beyond my reach'. This is the intro that sets up the gacha mechanic; the only mote in reach is the workshop perk Joe starts with.
'as my reach grew for the final grasp. A mote swung close from one of the mid-sized constellations.' Direct roll attempt prose.
'My power tried to grasp it, but the energy was too much. The flare of energy spun away.' Joe's first explicit miss.
Same miss as above; regex caught it twice via two trigger phrases. Dedup needed when computing final roll counts.
'Another mote would come, and I would be able to handle a stronger power by the time it arrived.' Anticipates next attempt.
'Metalwork Workshop which was from the Toolkits constellation' — names the constellation of Joe's starting perk.
'Personal affection on his part wasn't enough for me to compromise myself.' Social/relationship context, not gacha. The phrase appears commonly in non-roll prose.
'A new constellation approached and my power was latching onto a mote from it. It turned out to be the Crafting constellation and the mote was Smithing.' Confirmed real.
Same Smithing roll; explicit constellation name reveal.
'I had also missed a connection to the quality constellation when we moved out.' Matches curator's ch20 'miss: Quality'.

'The Magic constellation passed me by without a connection.' Matches curator's 'miss: Magic'. Note: regex tagged this as roll_attempt but it's actually a miss. Pattern: 'X constellation passed ... without a connection' is a miss flag.
'I felt the Celestial Forge make another connection to the Crafting constellation.' Matches curator's 'roll: Crafting' (Master Craftsman).
'the Celestial Forge missed a connection with the Knowledge constellation.' Matches curator's 'miss: Knowledge'.
Second Crafting connect (Engineer perk). Matches curator's second 'roll: Crafting' in this chapter.
'I felt the Forge connect to a familiar mote from the Quality constellation' — Joe rolling a repeat Minor Blessing or Unnatural Skill. Both Quality acquisitions are listed in this chapter.
'The Magic constellation passed by as I picked my way through the crowd' — Magic miss; matches curator's 'miss: Magic'.
Second Magic miss in same chapter — 'I paused as the Magic constellation passed by.' Curator log shows 2 Magic misses; both confirmed.
'a connection had been made to a mid-sized mote from the Toolkits constellation called the Launderer System.' Direct match to acquisition.
'I had felt my reach build through my conversation with Tattletale' — preludes the big Incredible Craftsmanship Quality roll (the chapter-titled 'Tattletale and Joe' moment).
'I felt the constellations shift as my power latched onto a cluster of Personal Reality motes.' Matches the cluster of PR perks acquired this chapter.
'as the Alchemy constellation missed a connection.' Matches curator's 'miss: Alchemy'.
'there just wasn't enough time to drill down into a new area of research.' Time-budget prose, not gacha.
'I felt the Forge fail to connect to a massive mote from the Knowledge constellation.' Explicit miss prose. Curator's roll log doesn't cover ch 92, but the regex correctly caught a Knowledge miss.

'I felt the Forge move again ... the largest amount I had ever assembled. As the Size constellation drifted towards me one of the remaining large motes was easily secured' — Cybertronian Forge acquisition (Size).

'there had still been power connected to that ceremony' — describing a Final Frontier ritual, NOT a gacha roll. The phrase 'power connected' is too generic; it triggers on any in-story power-related connection.
'New technologies and scientific principles were folded into the ship as I gained access to them.' Prose recap of capability growth, not a single roll. Bare 'I gained' too generic.
'In exchange I received skills in Weapons, Espionage, Communications, and Piloting.' Confirms the 4 Star Trek Skills paid acquisitions for this chapter (matches obtained_perks exactly).
'I felt a connection form to the Knowledge constellation.' Earlier of the two Knowledge mentions in this chapter — preludes the 4-Skills cluster acquisition.
'a cluster of motes that ... in the Knowledge constellation' — same roll, named twice; needs dedup.
'as the Personal Reality constellation missed a connection.' Real miss.
'Even that wasn't enough to guarantee success.' Plot prose about exploration, not gacha.
'There wasn't enough bandwidth to have a second person connect through the link.' Engineering prose, not gacha.

high_precision_phrases: missed a connection (98%+ real, in some form)
constellation passed (often 'X constellation passed' = real miss)
the X constellation (where X is one of the 14, near a verb like 'connected', 'passed', 'approached')
noisy_phrases: wasn't enough (very high false-positive rate; common in non-gacha prose)
I gained (too generic; matches narrative summaries)
power connected (triggers on tinker / passenger / magic prose, not just CF)
my reach (real in setup chapters; sometimes about literal reach later)
patterns_observed.double_counting: When a single roll is described in multiple paragraphs, regex catches multiple phrases. Need offset-window deduplication (e.g. collapse hits within 200 chars of each other).
patterns_observed.miss_vs_attempt_confusion: candidate_kind='roll_attempt' for 'X constellation passed' is misleading - that pattern almost always indicates a MISS. Should be reclassified.
patterns_observed.post_curator_coverage: Chapters 92+ have no curator rolls.json log, so regex is the only signal. Chapter 92's hits look clean (1 miss + 1 hit), 100's are noisier (3 false positives among 11 hits).

next_steps: Tighten 'wasn't enough', 'I gained', 'power connected' patterns or drop them entirely.
Add offset-window dedup (collapse hits within ~200 chars).
Use the constellation_reveal hit as the primary roll-event anchor; nearby roll_attempt/miss hits can be folded into it.
Validate the cleaned regex against the curator's 1-75 rolls.json — should match >90% of curator events.
